figures.caption: Figure 1: Deformation of Flexible beam : (a) initial mesh (1980 triangles). (b) deformed mesh (mesh topology and the number of triangles remain the same).

Figure 9: The verification of the superposition principle of the surrogate model: (a) is the deformed mesh generated by the NACA 0012 airfoil after rotation and translation superposition under the standard scale (a=1, b=1). (b) is the superposition error under the standard scale.

Figure 2: Element quality metrics of flexible beam distorted from the top and bottom layers of beam: (a) the deformed mesh generated by FEM. (b) the deformed mesh generated by the proposed surrogate-model.

Figure 3: The proposed surrogate model for the solution of Eq. (1) in this case : (a) the solution in X direction. (b) the solution in Y direction. Both evaluate the approximation accuracy by the point-wise squared error.

Figure 8: The verification of the scale invariance of the flexible beam example: (a) The deformed mesh used as the scale reference. (b) The quantitative relationship between the scale scaling factor and output response.

Figure 7: The verification of the scale invariance of the NACA 0012 airfoil example: (a) The deformed mesh used as the scale reference. (b) The quantitative relationship between the scale scaling factor and output response.
Figure 4: Deformation of NACA 0012 airfoil : (a) initial mesh (2603 triangles). (b) deformed mesh.

Figure 5: Element quality metrics of NACA 0012 airfoil under combined translational and rotational rigid-body motion: (a) deformed mesh generated by FEM. (b) deformed mesh generated by the proposed surrogate model.
Table 3: Relative Superposition Error (RSE) for Different Superposition Parameter Combinations

Figure 6: The proposed surrogate model for the solution of Eq. (1) in this case : (a) the solution in X direction. (b) the solution in Y direction. Both evaluate the approximation accuracy by the point-wise squared error.
